blog_articles.title: Smart Layout Ideas for Small Apartments
blog_articles.excerpt: Make every square foot count with flexible furniture, smart storage, and light-first layouts tailored to compact apartments.
blog_articles.content:
Designing a small apartment is a puzzle of light, circulation, and storage. In this guide, we break down four layout strategies that work in spaces under 500 sq ft: the corridor-free plan, the double-duty wall, the flexible living core, and the layered lighting approach. We also show real before-and-after diagrams from client projects, including how we carved out a proper dining nook in just 36 inches of depth.

Start by mapping your natural light and deciding which functions deserve the brightest zone—usually living or working. Then anchor storage along the darkest wall. Opt for furniture with exposed legs to keep the floor visible and maintain the feeling of openness. Finally, use rugs to define zones instead of walls.

blog_articles.title: Hidden Storage Tricks for Tiny City Apartments
blog_articles.excerpt: Turn awkward corners and dead zones into beautiful, hard-working storage in your studio or one-bedroom.
blog_articles.content:
Storage is the number one challenge our small-apartment clients mention. Instead of bulky wardrobes, we favor shallow built-ins, over-door cabinets, and under-seat storage that blends into the design. In this article we cover measurement guidelines, ideal depths for different types of storage, and how to choose finishes that visually recede.

We share three real-life case studies, including a 340 sq ft rental where we added 90 cubic feet of storage without blocking a single window.

blog_articles.title: Using Color to Open Up a Small Apartment
blog_articles.excerpt: The right palette can make a 350 sq ft apartment feel airy instead of cramped.
blog_articles.content: Color impacts how spacious your home feels. We explore light-reflective neutrals, accent walls, and tone-on-tone schemes that keep visual noise low while still feeling warm and personal. We also share paint and fabric combinations that work especially well in small city apartments.

blog_articles.title: How to Plan a Realistic Budget for Your Kitchen Renovation
blog_articles.excerpt: From cabinetry to countertops, we break down where your kitchen budget really goes.
blog_articles.content: Kitchen renovations are one of the most expensive projects in a home. In this guide, we benchmark costs for cabinetry, appliances, countertops, lighting, and labor at three budget levels. We also outline where it makes sense to splurge, where to save, and how our studio structures fixed-fee vs per-room pricing for kitchen design packages.

blog_articles.title: Designing an Industrial-Style Home Office that Still Feels Warm
blog_articles.excerpt: Concrete, metal, and exposed brick can feel cozy with the right balance of texture and light.
blog_articles.content: Industrial spaces are known for raw materials and generous ceilings, but without careful layering they can feel cold. We break down how to bring warmth through wood tones, textiles, and accent lighting while keeping the industrial character intact. We include a detailed breakdown of one of our recent industrial office projects, from early concept sketches to final styling.

blog_articles.title: Scandinavian Living Rooms: 5 Client Before-and-Afters
blog_articles.excerpt: See how we transformed dark, cramped living rooms into light-filled Scandinavian spaces.
blog_articles.content: From cluttered rentals to family living rooms, Scandinavian design principles help us create calm, light, and welcoming spaces. This article walks through five different living room transformations, including budget and timeline details for each. If you are considering a Scandinavian refresh under $12,000, pay attention to how we prioritized flooring, paint, and key furniture pieces.

blog_articles.title: A Minimalist Design Roadmap for 2-Room Apartments
blog_articles.excerpt: Simplify your 2-room apartment without losing comfort or personality.
blog_articles.content: Minimalism in small multi-room apartments is about clarity, not emptiness. We show you how to set a tight color palette, control visual noise, and choose multi-purpose furniture that supports your routines. We reference one of our favorite two-room minimalist apartments, including its budget range of $15,000–$20,000 and a 10-week timeline.

blog_articles.title: Budget-Friendly Bedroom Refreshes Under $8,000
blog_articles.excerpt: See how far a modest budget can go in transforming a tired bedroom into a restful retreat.
blog_articles.content: With a clear scope and smart priorities, $5,000–$8,000 is enough to completely reimagine a bedroom. We detail three client projects—including a 110 sq ft city bedroom—covering furniture, textiles, lighting, and light construction work like wall repair and paint.

blog_articles.title: Mixing Modern and Bohemian Styles Without the Clutter
blog_articles.excerpt: Layer texture and pattern over a clean modern base for a balanced Modern + Boho look.
blog_articles.content: Modern and Bohemian styles may seem opposite, but they can complement each other beautifully. We start with a modern foundation—clean lines, simple silhouettes, and a restrained color palette—then layer in Bohemian elements through textiles, art, and collected objects. The key is editing: for every bold piece you add, remove something visually heavy.

blog_articles.title: How Our Design Fees and Payment Schedules Work
blog_articles.excerpt: Understand our fixed-fee packages, per-room pricing, deposits, and installment options.
blog_articles.content: We aim to keep pricing transparent and predictable. This article explains how we structure deposits, milestone payments, and optional installment plans across e-design, consultation, and full-service packages. We also link directly to our current Services & Pricing overview so you can compare packages side by side.

faq_items.question: Do you offer payment plans or installments?
faq_items.answerHtml:
Yes. For most full-service design packages and larger multi-room projects, we offer installment-based payment plans. Typically, the design fee is split into three parts: a 40% deposit to reserve your start date, 40% at concept approval, and 20% at final delivery.

For certain packages, you can opt into monthly installments billed to a credit or debit card. To see which packages qualify and compare exact amounts, please See detailed pricing options.

faq_items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
faq_items.answerHtml:
We accept major credit cards, debit cards, ACH bank transfers, and international wire transfers for larger projects. For local clients, we also accept checks for deposits and final balances.

All payments are processed through secure, PCI-compliant providers. We do not store your full card number on our servers.

faq_items.question: How are your design fees structured?
faq_items.answerHtml:
Most of our services are offered as fixed-fee packages based on the type of space (kitchen, living room, office, etc.) and the level of service (e-design vs. full-service). Some services use per-room pricing, while others are priced as a flat project fee.

You can always see current package pricing and inclusions on our Services & Pricing page.

faq_items.question: What is your refund and cancellation policy?
faq_items.answerHtml:
Design retainers and deposits are non-refundable once your project start date is reserved. If you need to reschedule, we can usually move your slot with at least 30 days' notice.

For e-design and consultation packages, you can cancel up to 48 hours before your scheduled session for a full refund. After deliverables have been shared, refunds are not available.

faq_items.question: How long does a typical project take from start to finish?
faq_items.answerHtml:
Most single-room e-design projects take 2–4 weeks. Full-service spaces like kitchens and bathrooms can take 2–6 months depending on contractor availability and material lead times.

On each project page we show a rough timeline category (for example, "1–3 months"). During your consultation, we'll provide a more precise estimate based on your scope.

faq_items.question: What is the difference between full-service design and e-design?
faq_items.answerHtml:
Full-service design is our most comprehensive offering. We handle site visits, measurements, trade coordination, ordering, and installation support. E-design is a remote, lower-cost service where we provide concepts, layouts, and shopping lists for you to implement.

Both options include custom design, but full-service is ideal if you want a done-for-you experience and construction is involved.

faq_items.question: Do all packages include 3D renderings?
faq_items.answerHtml:
No. 3D renderings are included with selected packages, typically mid-tier and premium full-service or e-design options. Our entry-level consultation services do not include 3D visuals.

When browsing the Services page, look for the "3D renderings" feature tag on each package card.

faq_items.question: Can you work with very small apartments or awkward layouts?
faq_items.answerHtml:
Absolutely. A large portion of our portfolio is made up of small city apartments, micro-studios, and irregular floor plans. We specialize in space planning that maximizes storage and light.

Browse our Projects and Blog sections for real examples of small-apartment transformations.

faq_items.question: How can I contact you if I have more questions?
faq_items.answerHtml: You can use the contact form on our Contact page, or email us directly at hello@example-studio.com. We typically respond within one business day.

faq_items.question: What information should I include in my project brief?
faq_items.answerHtml: Your brief should include the type of space (e.g., living room, kitchen, office), approximate size, number of rooms, your target budget, and your ideal timeline. It also helps to share a few inspiration images or links that reflect your style.

inspiration_images.description: Clean-lined sofa, low media unit, and a single sculptural floor lamp in a bright modern living room.

inspiration_images.title: Calm Modern Bedroom

inspiration_images.title: Layered Bohemian Lounge
inspiration_images.description: Patterned rug, abundant plants, and collected textiles create a relaxed Boho living area.

inspiration_images.title: Warm Bohemian Bedroom
inspiration_images.description: Earth-toned bedding, woven pendants, and a vintage rug in a cozy Bohemian bedroom.

inspiration_images.title: Soft Scandinavian Living Room
inspiration_images.description: Pale wood floors, light gray sofa, and simple black accents in a bright Scandinavian space.

inspiration_images.title: Minimalist Two-Room Apartment
inspiration_images.description: Open-plan living and dining zone with a pared-back palette and minimal furniture.

inspiration_images.title: Industrial Loft Office
inspiration_images.description: Exposed brick, black-framed glazing, and a large shared worktable in an industrial office.

inspiration_images.title: Classic White Kitchen
inspiration_images.description: Panelled cabinetry, marble counters, and brass hardware in a timeless kitchen.

inspiration_images.title: Eclectic Patterned Lounge
inspiration_images.description: Mismatched seating, bold art, and layered textiles for a collected feel.

inspiration_images.title: Compact Entryway with Bench
inspiration_images.description: Simple bench, hooks, and mirror creating a functional entry zone in a small apartment.

projects.title: Brooklyn Nook – Scandinavian Living Room Refresh
projects.summary: A 260 sq ft Brooklyn living room transformed into a light, airy Scandinavian retreat on a tight budget.
projects.description: This rental living room had great windows but felt dark and cluttered. We kept the existing layout but swapped heavy furniture for light, leggy pieces, introduced a pale oak media unit, and layered in linen textiles. With a budget under $8,000, we focused on paint, key furniture, and lighting, leaving the floor and built-ins intact.

projects.title: Sunlit Suite – Scandinavian Living Room Makeover
projects.summary: An airy Scandinavian-inspired living room for a young couple, completed under a $12,000 budget.
projects.description: This corner-unit apartment had great light but no storage. We designed a custom low media wall, added a slimline bookcase, and introduced tone-on-tone textiles in wool and cotton. The palette is all about soft whites, warm wood, and a few black accents.

projects.title: Family Lounge – Scandinavian Living Room for Four
projects.summary: A family-friendly Scandinavian living room with durable fabrics and lots of storage.
projects.description: For this young family, we created a flexible living space with a sectional sofa, kid-friendly storage, and a large rug that anchors the room. Built-ins around the TV hide toys and media, while still looking calm and grown-up.

projects.title: Calm Lines – Minimalist 2-Room Apartment
projects.summary: A 2-room city apartment stripped back to essentials with a total budget of $18,000.
projects.description: Our client wanted a calm, minimalist home with just enough warmth for guests. We re-planned the living and bedroom zones, introduced built-in storage, and specified a tight palette of white, sand, and charcoal. The project was completed within a $15,000–$20,000 budget range.

projects.title: Soft Minimal – 2-Room Apartment With Hidden Storage
projects.summary: A minimalist 2-room apartment with extensive built-in storage and a soft neutral palette.
projects.description: For this 2-room apartment, we wrapped two walls with shallow built-ins and added a sliding panel to conceal the TV. The result is a serene home with everything tucked away but easy to access.

projects.title: Startup Loft – Industrial Office in a Former Warehouse
projects.summary: An industrial loft office for a tech startup, completed in under three months.
projects.description: We converted an old warehouse floor into a flexible office with breakout zones, a central meeting pod, and generous storage. Exposed brick and ductwork keep the character, while acoustic panels and soft seating add comfort.

projects.title: Media Agency Hub – Compact Industrial Office
projects.summary: A compact industrial office with hot-desking, lounge seating, and a content studio.
projects.description: This 900 sq ft space had to accommodate editing bays, meeting space, and casual seating. We used glass partitions, movable screens, and robust finishes suited to daily content production.

projects.title: Makers Space – Industrial Office and Workshop
projects.summary: A hybrid industrial office and prototyping workshop with robust materials and flexible zones.
projects.description: We carved out quiet workstations, a materials library, and an open workshop inside a former factory unit. Durable flooring, ample storage, and bright task lighting make the space hardworking yet inviting.

projects.title: Enterprise Hub – Industrial Corporate Office
projects.summary: A large industrial-inspired corporate office with multiple neighborhoods and meeting types.
projects.description: This project brought industrial character into a polished corporate environment. We used a restrained palette of oak, black metal, and soft textiles over exposed concrete floors.

projects.title: Open Plan – Modern Kitchen and Dining
projects.summary: A bright, modern kitchen opened to the dining area with generous storage and seating.
projects.description: We removed a dividing wall to connect the kitchen and dining spaces, then installed custom cabinetry, an island with seating, and layered lighting. The finishes are classic white with warm wood accents and matte black hardware.

projects.title: Compact Comfort – Scandinavian Kitchen
projects.summary: A compact Scandinavian kitchen with clever storage and a cozy breakfast bar.
projects.description: This small kitchen needed both storage and personality. We specified flat-front white cabinetry, oak open shelves, and a slim peninsula to create a breakfast spot without crowding the room.

projects.title: Heritage Home – Traditional Kitchen Renovation
projects.summary: A full-gut kitchen renovation in a historic home with classic details and modern functionality.
projects.description: We respected the home's original character with inset cabinetry, detailed millwork, and classic materials, while updating the layout for modern cooking and entertaining.

projects.title: City Sleep – Budget Bedroom Refresh
projects.summary: A 110 sq ft bedroom refresh completed for $5,000 with a focus on storage and calm.
projects.description: This small rental bedroom relied on mismatched furniture and lacked storage. We introduced a storage bed, slimline wardrobe, and soft layered textiles to make the space restful and functional.

projects.title: Cozy Attic – Sloped-Ceiling Bedroom
projects.summary: A sloped-ceiling attic bedroom redesigned with low furniture and warm textures.
projects.description: We embraced the low ceilings and angles by using floor-hugging furniture and a layered rug strategy. Warm neutrals and a simple layout keep the room feeling calm.

projects.title: Guest Suite – Versatile Bedroom and Work Corner
projects.summary: A guest-ready bedroom with a compact work corner and generous storage, all under $8,000.
projects.description: We created a welcoming space for guests that doubles as a part-time home office. A daybed with trundle, small desk, and wall-mounted storage keep the room flexible.

projects.title: Skyline Suite – Luxury Master Bedroom
projects.summary: A luxurious master bedroom with custom millwork and a hotel-inspired palette.
projects.description: This master suite makeover included custom wardrobes, an upholstered wall, and integrated lighting. The result feels like a boutique hotel suite.

service_packages.name: Essential Full-Service Design with 3D
service_packages.description: Our entry full-service design package for single spaces. Includes on-site visits, detailed floor plans, mood boards, and 3D renderings.

service_packages.name: Premium Full-Service Design with 3D
service_packages.description: Comprehensive full-service design with multiple 3D views, detailed elevations, and full procurement support.

service_packages.name: Signature Full-Home Design with 3D
service_packages.description: Our most in-depth package for multi-room or full-home projects, with extensive 3D visualization and on-site styling.

service_packages.name: Standard Full-Service Design (No 3D)
service_packages.description: A streamlined full-service package without 3D renderings, ideal for straightforward spaces.

service_packages.name: Basic E-Design – Single Room
service_packages.description: A remote e-design package for one room. Includes a mood board, simple floor plan, and curated shopping list you can implement at your own pace.

service_packages.name: Plus E-Design – Single Room with 3D
service_packages.description: A more detailed remote e-design package for one room, including 3D renderings and two revision rounds.

service_packages.name: Remote Design Consultation (90 Minutes)
service_packages.description: A focused video consultation to review your space, answer questions, and outline next steps.

service_packages.name: In-Home Design Consultation
service_packages.description: An on-site working session to walk through your space, take measurements, and discuss ideas.

service_packages.name: Mini Styling Refresh
service_packages.description: A quick styling package focused on decor, textiles, and simple layout tweaks for one room.

service_packages.name: Full-Home Styling Package
service_packages.description: Coordinated styling across multiple rooms, including accessory selection, art placement, and on-site styling day.

testimonials.title: Our Open-Plan Kitchen Finally Matches How We Live
testimonials.summary: They turned a dark, closed-off kitchen into the bright, social hub of our home.
testimonials.fullText: We had dreamed about opening our kitchen to the dining area for years but were overwhelmed by the decisions. The studio walked us through every step  from structural considerations to outlet placement. The 3D visuals helped us commit to removing the wall, and the final result looks exactly like the renderings. Friends always comment on how bright and spacious it feels now. Cooking, homework, and entertaining all happen in one connected space, and the storage solutions mean our counters are finally clear.

testimonials.title: A Tiny Kitchen That Works Like a Much Bigger One
testimonials.summary: Clever storage and a simple Scandinavian palette made our small kitchen a joy to use.
testimonials.fullText: Our galley kitchen in the apartment was cramped and cluttered. The team managed to add a breakfast bar, more counter space, and better lighting without making it feel tight. The Scandinavian-inspired finishes are calm and bright, and we finally have a place to sit with coffee in the mornings. They respected our budget and helped us choose where to invest (cabinets and lighting) and where to save.

testimonials.title: A Classic Kitchen That Still Feels Fresh
testimonials.summary: They honored the character of our old house while giving us a highly functional kitchen.
testimonials.fullText: We were nervous about renovating our kitchen in a 1920s home. The designers understood exactly how to keep the traditional feel with inset cabinetry and detailed trim while improving storage and workflow. The end result feels like it belongs in the house but functions like a new build. Their guidance on finishes and hardware kept us on track and within the agreed budget.

testimonials.title: Our Small Brooklyn Living Room Feels Twice the Size
testimonials.summary: Thoughtful Scandinavian touches made our 260 sq ft living room feel light and open.
testimonials.fullText: We assumed our rental living room was just too small to feel comfortable. The studio proved us wrong with a simple, clever layout and pieces that dont overwhelm the room. The pale woods, soft textiles, and better lighting make the space feel calm instead of cramped. They respected our under-$8,000 budget and reused a few existing pieces in smart ways.

testimonials.title: A Calm, Scandinavian Living Room We Love Coming Home To
testimonials.summary: Custom storage and a soft palette transformed a cluttered space into a serene retreat.
testimonials.fullText: Our living room had plenty of light but no storage, so it always looked messy. The team designed a low media wall and slim bookcase that hide the clutter and display just enough favorite objects. The Scandinavian palette of whites, warm woods, and a few black accents works with everything we own. The project stayed under our $12,000 budget, and the process felt organized and transparent from start to finish.

testimonials.title: A Family Living Room That Finally Works for Everyone
testimonials.summary: They created a Scandinavian-inspired space that is both kid-friendly and grown-up.
testimonials.fullText: With two young kids, our living room had become a toy explosion. The designers planned built-ins around the TV that hide toys behind doors and drawers, plus open shelves for books and decor. The big rug and durable sofa fabric have held up incredibly well. We now have a room that feels calm for adults but still works for playtime and movie nights.

testimonials.title: An Industrial Loft Office That Reflects Our Startup Culture
testimonials.summary: They turned a raw warehouse floor into a flexible, inspiring workspace in 10 weeks.
testimonials.fullText: We wanted an office that felt distinctly "us" without looking like every other startup. The team embraced the industrial bones of the building  brick walls, high ceilings, exposed ductwork  and layered in warm textures and acoustic solutions so it stays comfortable and quiet enough to work. The breakout zones and central meeting pod get used every single day, and visitors always compliment the design.

testimonials.title: A Compact Office That Does Everything We Need
testimonials.summary: Hot desks, a lounge, and a small studio all fit comfortably into 900 sq ft.
testimonials.fullText: As a content agency, we needed spaces for quiet editing, quick stand-ups, and casual client meetings in a very small footprint. The designers used glass partitions and movable screens so the space can shift during the day. The industrial style feels creative but not gimmicky. The project was completed quickly and on schedule, which mattered a lot to us.

testimonials.title: A Hardworking Office and Workshop in One
testimonials.summary: They balanced industrial durability with comfort for our hybrid office and prototyping lab.
testimonials.fullText: We run a small hardware startup, so our space has to function as both an office and a workshop. The team carved out quiet desks, a materials library, and a flexible build area without the space feeling chopped up. Durable finishes and plenty of storage keep things tidy, and the lighting is bright where it needs to be and softer in the lounge area. It has completely changed how we work.

testimonials.title: A $5,000 Bedroom Makeover That Feels Priceless
testimonials.summary: They transformed our tiny bedroom with smart storage and soothing colors.
testimonials.fullText: Our 110 sq ft bedroom used to be full of mismatched pieces and overflowing drawers. With a clear plan and a tight budget, the team recommended a storage bed, slim wardrobe, and a simple color palette that makes the room feel twice as calm. We did not realize how much the bedroom affected our sleep until now. The fact that everything stayed within our $5,000 budget was a huge relief.

testimonials.title: Turning a Tricky Attic into a Cozy Bedroom
testimonials.summary: They embraced the sloped ceilings and made the room feel intimate instead of awkward.
testimonials.fullText: We didnt know what to do with our attic because of the low, sloping ceilings. The designers created a floor-hugging layout with layered rugs and warm textures that makes the space feel intentional and inviting. The new lighting plan is a game changer; there are no more dark corners. Its now our favorite room in the house.

testimonials.title: A Minimalist Apartment That Still Feels Warm
testimonials.summary: They edited our 2-room apartment down to essentials without making it feel empty.
testimonials.fullText: We asked for a minimalist home but were worried it might end up feeling cold. The team found the right balance: a very tight color palette, clean-lined furniture, and just enough texture to keep things comfortable. Built-in storage along one wall hides all the everyday clutter. The project respected our $18,000 budget and wrapped up within the promised 10-week timeline.
